commands.repair_reason: Contributor or maintainer follow-up is needed to repair the broad account-preservation behavior and supply real WeChat proof; ClawSweeper automation should not replace the missing external setup evidence.; Needs attention: The diff can keep a removed channel account running across hot reload, so account removal is not reliably enforced as an operator control boundary.; Review finding: [P1] Limit known-account restar... (sha=fcdf0656ef307e4ae2cc3388b51e97c46d7f47ca)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a gateway `startChannel` option to include already-known channel accounts during hot reload, calls it from reload restart paths, updates WeChat docs, and adds gateway regression tests.

PR surface: Source +55, Tests +322, Docs +2. Total +379 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the patch risk by source inspection: a non-plugin channel config reload stops with `manual: false`, then the PR restarts with `includeKnownAccounts`, and the PR's stop path marks those accounts `restartPending`. The original live WeChat A+B flow was not reproduced here; the linked issue supplies logs, while the PR proof is mocked tests only.
commands.review_followup: Contributor or maintainer follow-up is needed to repair the broad account-preservation behavior and supply real WeChat proof; ClawSweeper automation should not replace the missing external setup evidence.

target.title: fix(wechat): preserve existing accounts across hot reload
target.body:
## Summary
- preserve known live accounts when a channel hot-reload restarts without an explicit account id
- treat hot-reload stop/restart as a manager-owned handoff so existing accounts restart instead of getting stuck in a manual-stop state
- document the WeChat multi-account behavior and cover the hot-reload path with regression tests

## Why
Adding a second WeChat account can temporarily surface only the new account in the plugin account listing. Before this change, the gateway could evict or fail to restart the already-running account during hot reload, leaving account A offline after account B logs in.

## Testing
- `pnpm install`
- `git diff --check`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE=0 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-channels.test.ts src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts src/gateway/server.reload.test.ts` (3 files, 63 tests passed)
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE=0 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/gateway-cli/run.option-collisions.test.ts` (1 file, 24 tests passed)

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: plugin hot reload for `openclaw-weixin` treats the pre-replace channel stop as a manager-owned handoff and restarts the active channel with known accounts included.

Real environment tested: clean temporary OpenClaw contributor worktree on Rohin's Mac mini at commit `fcdf0656ef`; Node v25.9.0; pnpm 11.2.2.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE=0 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-channels.test.ts src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts src/gateway/server.reload.test.ts`.

Evidence after fix: the gateway hot-reload regression suite passed with 3 test files and 63 tests, including the plugin pre-replace rollback and deferred channel reload paths that restart with `{ includeKnownAccounts: true }`.

Observed result after fix: the hot-reload path calls `stopChannel("openclaw-weixin", undefined, { manual: false })` before plugin replacement, then `startChannel("openclaw-weixin", undefined, { includeKnownAccounts: true })` after replacement, so the manager-owned reload path does not mark the account as manually stopped before account preservation runs.

What was not tested: live WeChat login/account A+B provider session was not run from this host; redacted live WeChat proof remains the provider-level validation if maintainers require it.

## AI assisted
- OpenAI Codex
target.author: newdldewdl
